title: Operationalize the sprint runner: scheduled nightly runs on the safe lane
component: scripts/sprint.py, tools/tools_scheduler.py or Windows Task Scheduler, logs/sprint/
current_state: sprint.py is complete (guardrails, god-path isolation, cost caps, branch-only commits) but has never run unattended on a schedule. T-169 covers proving ONE honest end-to-end run; nothing schedules it afterward.
target_state: After T-169 proves a real closure: a nightly scheduled run (--auto-implement, --max-tickets 1, --max-cost 0.50) against safe-component tickets only; morning Telegram summary (ticket picked, branch, verify result, or 'nothing safe in queue'). Ash reviews branches over coffee. Kill switch: a sprint.disabled flag file stops all runs.
migration_plan: 1. Wrapper scripts/sprint_nightly.py: checks sprint.disabled, runs sprint.py with the conservative flags, captures exit + log dir, sends the Telegram summary.
2. Schedule via Windows Task Scheduler (documented schtasks one-liner Ash runs himself) — NOT inside the agent process; the runner must work with Pi offline.
3. Failure budget: 3 consecutive escalations/failures → auto-create sprint.disabled + loud Telegram alert (runaway protection).
4. Tests: wrapper unit tests with mocked subprocess (disabled-flag respected, failure budget trips, summary format).
risk_notes: Unattended autonomy — every guardrail already in sprint.py stays load-bearing (god-path scanner, RISK_FLAGGED escalation, branch-only). The disabled-flag + failure budget are the new safety. Never let it touch main.
